A training workshop for officials from m: A training workshop for officials from major cancer treatment facilities and tertiary care centres in Bhubaneswar and Cuttack was conducted from 27th January to 3rd February 2026. The workshop was also attended by the newly recruited OSPBCR team, comprising one State Coordinator, five Zonal Coordinators, and one Programmer, with a total of 40 participants taking part in the programme.

All cancer-treating hospitals, District: All cancer-treating hospitals, District Headquarters Hospitals (DHHs), Primary Health Centres (PHCs), and other healthcare facilities involved in the diagnosis and treatment of cancer patients in Odisha are responsible for entering patient information into the Setu platform. This coordinated reporting mechanism supports comprehensive statewide cancer registration and strengthens the registry's ability to generate reliable estimates of the cancer burden.

Registry data collection and abstraction: Registry data collection and abstraction are carried out using the web-based Setu application, which incorporates a digitized version of the standardized registry proforma. The one-page proforma has been designed to capture essential variables that are routinely available during data collection, thereby promoting efficient and accurate reporting.

The Odisha State Population Based Cancer: The Odisha State Population Based Cancer Registry (OSPBCR) has developed a standardized Patient Information Form to facilitate systematic collection of cancer-related data across the state. The form serves as the primary tool for recording demographic, clinical, diagnostic, treatment, and follow-up information required for population-based cancer registration.

The Odisha State Population Based Cancer Registry (OSPBCR) is an initiative of the Department of Health and Family Welfare, Government of Odisha, to systematically document the cancer burden across the state, covering a population of 45 million people. The registry is being implemented from 2026 onwards with technical support from Tata Memorial Centre, Mumbai, and the National Institute for Science Education and Research (NISER), Jatni, Odisha.

The Odisha State Population Based Cancer Registry (OSPBCR) is an initiative of the Department of Health and Family Welfare, Government of Odisha, to systematically document the cancer burden across the state, covering a population of 45 million people. The registry is being implemented from 2026 onwards with technical support from Tata Memorial Centre, Mumbai, and the National Institute for Science Education and Research, Jatni, Odisha.
The Odisha State Population-Based Cancer: The Odisha State Population-Based Cancer Registry (OSPBCR) is an initiative of the Department of Health and Family Welfare, Government of Odisha, established to systematically collect and manage cancer data across the state. The registry aims to strengthen cancer surveillance and support evidence-based planning for cancer prevention and control.
The SOP serves as a reference manual for: The SOP serves as a reference manual for all personnel involved in cancer registration across the thirty districts of Odisha, ensuring standardized procedures for data collection, case abstraction, coding, and reporting. It aims to maintain high-quality, complete, and consistent cancer registry data across all participating centres.
The Setu web-based application has been: The Setu web-based application has been developed as the official data management platform for the OSPBCR. It facilitates standardized collection, storage, and management of cancer registry data, ensuring uniform reporting and improved data quality across participating institutions.
The Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) h: The Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) has been developed to provide uniform guidelines for the implementation of the Odisha State Population Based Cancer Registry (OSPBCR). The registry is an initiative of the Department of Health and Family Welfare, Government of Odisha, with technical support from Tata Memorial Centre (TMC), Mumbai, and the National Institute of Science Education and Research (NISER), Jatni, Odisha.
The form captures essential patient deta: The form captures essential patient details, including demographic characteristics, contact information, residential location, hospital details, and Aadhaar number to support accurate identification and follow-up. Clinical information collected includes the date and basis of diagnosis, primary site of cancer, stage at diagnosis, histological findings, and ICD-O-3-based classification to ensure standardized reporting.
The manual provides detailed guidance on: The manual provides detailed guidance on each data variable collected by the registry and supports registry staff in maintaining uniform data recording practices. Users are encouraged to study the SOP thoroughly before commencing registry activities and to seek guidance from supervisors and Tata Memorial Centre experts whenever technical clarification or operational support is required.
The standardized data collection format: The standardized data collection format supports uniform reporting across participating healthcare facilities, improves data quality and completeness, and strengthens the accuracy of cancer incidence, treatment, and survival estimates. It provides the foundation for evidence-based cancer surveillance, research, programme planning, and policy development under the Odisha State Population Based Cancer Registry.
The training covered core concepts of ca: The training covered core concepts of cancer registration, case abstraction procedures, ICD-O-3 coding, use of the "Setu" web-based data management tool, follow-up techniques, and the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) of the PBCR. Practical demonstrations were conducted by the staff of Khordha PBCR, and pre- and post-tests indicated a strong understanding of registry-related concepts among the participants and their readiness to support registry activities.
The training covered the fundamental pri: The training covered the fundamental principles of population-based cancer registration, case abstraction procedures, ICD-O-3 coding, use of the "SETU" web-based cancer registration system, follow-up techniques, and the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for PBCR implementation. Expert faculty delivered lectures on cancer management and clinical terminologies to strengthen participants' understanding of data abstraction, while practical demonstrations were conducted by the Khordha PBCR team. Pre- and post-training assessments indicated a strong understanding of registry-related concepts and readiness among participants to support registry activities.
The workshop coincided with the observan: The workshop coincided with the observance of World Cancer Day 2026, during which the Department of Health and Family Welfare, Government of Odisha, officially launched the Odisha State Population Based Cancer Registry at the National Institute of Science Education and Research (NISER), Bhubaneswar. The programme was inaugurated by Dr. Nilakantha Mishra, Director of Public Health, Government of Odisha, and was presided over by Dr. A. Srinivasan, Dean of Faculty Affairs, NISER, marking an important milestone in strengthening cancer surveillance across the state.
To support evidence-based public health: To support evidence-based public health planning and policy decisions.
Training workshops for district health o: Training workshops for district health officials from all 30 districts of Odisha were conducted in two batches, with the first batch held from 7th to 9th January 2026 for 16 districts and the second batch from 12th to 14th January 2026 for the remaining 14 districts. A total of 115 participants, including 26 District Public Health Officers (DPHO) and Additional District Public Health Officers (ADPHO), attended the workshops.
